message:
fix(stack/agent-mail): tighten readiness regex and harden symlink repair

Two related hardenings around the MCP Agent Mail service-bring-up path
discovered while debugging install-time flakes.

1. Readiness regex tightening
   `install.sh`, `scripts/lib/stack.sh::_stack_agent_mail_readiness`,
   and the inline readiness probe emitted by stack.sh into the
   generated MCP Agent Mail health-check script all matched the
   service's `/health` JSON with:

       grep -Eq '"status"[[:space:]]*:[[:space:]]*"ready"'

   That regex matches ANY status field whose value *starts with*
   `ready` — `"ready"`, `"ready_to_warm"`, `"ready_for_writes"`,
   `"readying"`, etc. would all pass. The service does not currently
   emit any of those, but a future status name starting with "ready"
   would silently turn into a green readiness signal even if the
   service was not actually serving requests.

   Tightens the regex to require the closing-quote / closing-comma /
   closing-brace JSON terminator after `"ready"`:

       grep -Eq '"status"[[:space:]]*:[[:space:]]*"ready"([[:space:]]*[,}])'

   - `[[:space:]]*` allows JSON whitespace between the value and the
     terminator.
   - `[,}]` accepts either the field separator (more siblings ahead)
     or the closing brace (this is the last field). Both are valid
     JSON terminators for a string value at the top level.
   - The closing quote of `"ready"` is already in the literal, so a
     longer "ready_..." value cannot satisfy the trailing
     `[[:space:]]*[,}]` requirement.

   Applied at all three sites so the readiness contract is consistent
   between install-time, runtime stack-doctor, and the per-service
   health probe shipped to systemd.

2. Symlink-repair simplification
   `_stack_repair_agent_mail_cli_symlink` previously did:

       mkdir -p $dir 2>/dev/null || true
       if [[ -w $dir || ! -e $am_dest ]]; then
           ln -sf $am_src $am_dest
       fi

   Two issues:
   - On a non-writable target dir with an existing non-symlink file
     at `$am_dest`, the helper printed nothing and silently returned
     "success" while leaving the broken state in place.
   - When `dir/am` resolves to the same path as `am_src` (the source
     of truth itself, e.g. when the canonical agent-mail bin
     directory is one of the iteration targets), `ln -sf` would
     replace the source binary with a symlink-to-itself if the
     filesystem allowed it.

   Replaces with:

       [[ "$dir/am" == "$am_src" ]] && continue
       ... mkdir -p $dir && ln -sfn $am_src $am_dest && [[ -x $am_dest ]]

   - Skips the iteration when the target IS the source.
   - `mkdir -p $dir` failing now propagates as a repair failure
     (caller flips `repaired=1`) instead of being swallowed by
     `|| true`.
   - `ln -sfn` (no-dereference) prevents `ln` from following an
     existing symlink at $am_dest and writing through to its target,
     which mattered when $am_dest pointed at the wrong asupersync
     binary inherited from a previous install.
   - The trailing `[[ -x $am_dest ]]` only succeeds when the symlink
     target is executable, so a dangling symlink (target removed
     between install steps) is reported as a repair failure instead
     of silent success.

files.patch:
@@ -6231,7 +6231,7 @@ UNIT_EOF
                         local am_waited=0
                         local am_max_wait=30
                         until curl -fsS --max-time 10 http://127.0.0.1:8765/health/liveness >/dev/null 2>&1 && \
-                              curl -fsS --max-time 10 http://127.0.0.1:8765/health 2>/dev/null | grep -Eq '"status"[[:space:]]*:[[:space:]]*"ready"'; do
+                              curl -fsS --max-time 10 http://127.0.0.1:8765/health 2>/dev/null | grep -Eq '"status"[[:space:]]*:[[:space:]]*"ready"([[:space:]]*[,}])'; do
                             if [[ "$am_waited" -ge "$am_max_wait" ]]; then
                                 log_error "MCP Agent Mail service did not become ready on http://127.0.0.1:8765 after ${am_max_wait}s"
                                 break
@@ -6240,7 +6240,7 @@ UNIT_EOF
                             am_waited=$((am_waited + 2))
                         done
                         if curl -fsS --max-time 10 http://127.0.0.1:8765/health/liveness >/dev/null 2>&1 && \
-                           curl -fsS --max-time 10 http://127.0.0.1:8765/health 2>/dev/null | grep -Eq '"status"[[:space:]]*:[[:space:]]*"ready"'; then
+                           curl -fsS --max-time 10 http://127.0.0.1:8765/health 2>/dev/null | grep -Eq '"status"[[:space:]]*:[[:space:]]*"ready"([[:space:]]*[,}])'; then
                             log_success "MCP Agent Mail service running on http://127.0.0.1:8765"
                             am_service_ready=true
                         else

@@ -586,13 +586,14 @@ _stack_repair_agent_mail_cli_symlink() {
     local repaired=0
     for dir in "${bin_dirs[@]}"; do
         [[ -n "$dir" ]] || continue
+        [[ "$dir/am" == "$am_src" ]] && continue
         local dir_q=""
         local am_src_q=""
         local am_dest_q=""
         printf -v dir_q '%q' "$dir"
         printf -v am_src_q '%q' "$am_src"
         printf -v am_dest_q '%q' "$dir/am"
-        _stack_run_as_user "mkdir -p $dir_q 2>/dev/null || true; if [[ -w $dir_q || ! -e $am_dest_q ]]; then ln -sf $am_src_q $am_dest_q; fi" || repaired=1
+        _stack_run_as_user "mkdir -p $dir_q && ln -sfn $am_src_q $am_dest_q && [[ -x $am_dest_q ]]" || repaired=1
     done
 
     return "$repaired"
@@ -606,7 +607,7 @@ _stack_agent_mail_liveness() {
 _stack_agent_mail_readiness() {
     local readiness_body=""
     readiness_body="$(_stack_curl -fsS --max-time 10 http://127.0.0.1:8765/health 2>/dev/null)" || return 1
-    printf '%s\n' "$readiness_body" | grep -Eq '"status"[[:space:]]*:[[:space:]]*"ready"'
+    printf '%s\n' "$readiness_body" | grep -Eq '"status"[[:space:]]*:[[:space:]]*"ready"([[:space:]]*[,}])'
 }
 
 # Run a command as target user
@@ -926,7 +927,7 @@ if ! curl -fsS --max-time 10 http://127.0.0.1:8765/health/liveness >/dev/null 2>
 fi
 
 readiness_body="\$(curl -fsS --max-time 10 http://127.0.0.1:8765/health 2>/dev/null)" || exit 1
-printf '%s\n' "\$readiness_body" | grep -Eq '"status"[[:space:]]*:[[:space:]]*"ready"' || exit 1
+printf '%s\n' "\$readiness_body" | grep -Eq '"status"[[:space:]]*:[[:space:]]*"ready"([[:space:]]*[,}])' || exit 1
 
 runtime_dir="/run/user/\$(id -u)"
 if [[ -d "\$runtime_dir" ]]; then
@@ -1291,7 +1292,7 @@ launch_agent_mail_fallback() {
     if {
         _stack_curl -fsS --max-time 5 http://127.0.0.1:8765/health/liveness >/dev/null 2>&1 || \
         _stack_curl -fsS --max-time 5 http://127.0.0.1:8765/healthz >/dev/null 2>&1;
-    } && _stack_curl -fsS --max-time 5 http://127.0.0.1:8765/health 2>/dev/null | grep -Eq '"status"[[:space:]]*:[[:space:]]*"ready"'; then
+    } && _stack_curl -fsS --max-time 5 http://127.0.0.1:8765/health 2>/dev/null | grep -Eq '"status"[[:space:]]*:[[:space:]]*"ready"([[:space:]]*[,}])'; then
         return 0
     fi
